summary: Large ftCommon CPU/AI decompilation pass for src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01. The PR replaced many /// # stubs with C, added stage/camera/vector includes, tightened header prototypes, and advanced common fighter AI logic around recovery destinations, stage/blast-zone checks, item/target selection, and CpuCmd command dispatch. decomp.dev reported +7324 matched bytes, 11 new 100% matches, 25 partial improvements, and one ftCo_800A2718 regression.

key_files.role: Main source expansion: +4922/-841 across 20 hunks, replacing many ftCo_800A/ftCo_800B placeholders and adding matching helpers/wrappers.
Prototype cleanup: +10/-10, changing several static declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or bool to concrete s32/bool/void/Fighter*/Item*/Vec3 signatures.

what_changed: Added includes needed by the new decompiled code: cm/camera.h, gr/grcastle.h, gr/grgreatbay.h, gr/grrcruise.h, and lb/lbvector.h.

Implemented/reworked stage-aware recovery and bounds helpers including ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2C80, ftCo_800A3908, ftCo_800A4038, ftCo_800A4768, ftCo_800A6700, ftCo_800A6A98, ftCo_800A6D2C, and ftCo_800A6FC4.

Refactored ftCo_800A2718 and its inlineL0/inlineL1 helpers into structured item/island and stage cases for STORY, ZEBES, and ONETT; the bot report later flagged this function as the only matching regression.

Added item and target selection logic such as ftCo_800A4BEC, ftCo_800A4E8C, ftCo_800A61D8, and ftCo_800A648C, using HSD_GObj_Entities fighter/item traversal, ally checks, item kind filters, and distance ranking.

Expanded CPU action/recovery routines and command dispatch around ftCo_800A75DC, ftCo_800A7AAC, ftCo_800A8210, ftCo_800A866C, ftCo_800A8940, ftCo_800ABBA8, ftCo_800ADE48, ftCo_800AE7AC, ftCo_800AEA8C, ftCo_800AECF0, ftCo_800AEFB8, ftCo_800AF290, ftCo_800B04DC, ftCo_800B17D0, ftCo_800B1AB8, ftCo_800B1EF0, ftCo_800B21C8, ftCo_800B24B8, ftCo_800B2790, ftCo_800B2AFC, and ftCo_800B33B0.

Added small matching wrappers/trampolines such as ftCo_800A1B38_noinline, ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline/noinline2, ftCo_800A2718_dontinline chains, ftCo_800A8DE4_noinline, ftCo_800AC30C_noinline, and ftCo_800B1DA0_noinline variants.

Updated ftCo_0A01.h declarations for ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2C80, ftCo_800A4768, ftCo_800A6A98, ftCo_800A6D2C, ftCo_800A6FC4, ftCo_800A8210, ftCo_800A866C, ftCo_800AEA8C, and ftCo_800AEFB8 to use concrete return and parameter types.
smart_moves: Converted decompiler-style goto-heavy placeholder logic into structured loops and switches while preserving stage-specific behavior and the branch shapes needed for matching.
Factored repeated special-floor checks through helpers such as ftCo_800A1B38 and ftCo_800A6A98_inline0, avoiding duplicated Big Blue/Inishie/Corneria/Venom floor filtering.

Used explicit stage exceptions in the recovery/bounds logic instead of generic collision checks only: Great Bay, Castle, Zebes, Rainbow Cruise, Big Blue, Icicle Mountain, Onett, and Story all appear in the new code paths.
Kept CPU command generation readable by using existing CpuCmd_* names for stick/buttons/waits while still adding PAD_STACK and wrapper functions where codegen required it.
Improved type knowledge in the header rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, which makes later call-site and struct-field work easier.
Preserved address-based function names and anonymous x1A88 fields where semantics were not proven, avoiding speculative naming while still making the code compile and match better.
decomp_lessons: ftCommon CPU recovery code is tightly coupled to stage, camera, blast-zone, island, floor, wall, and item systems; matching these functions often requires adding the real cross-system headers rather than hiding calls behind placeholders.
Out-of-bounds and recovery-destination checks need both stage-specific exceptions and generic Camera_8003118C/Stage_GetBlastZone* fallbacks.
For CPU AI routines, type cleanup matters: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with Fighter*, Item*, Vec3*, bool, s32, and void clarifies control flow and downstream matching.
Tiny noinline/dontinline trampolines can be legitimate matching tools when otherwise identical helper calls inline differently at specific call sites.
HSD_GObj_Entities traversal plus GET_FIGHTER/GET_ITEM and ally/disabled-state filters are reusable patterns for CPU target and item selection.

Bot reports should be read per-function: this PR made large net progress but still introduced a localized ftCo_800A2718 regression, so future passes should check both matched gains and partial regressions.
naming_conventions: Kept existing address-based names for unknown functions: ftCo_800Axxxx and ftCo_800Bxxxx.
Used suffixes such as _noinline, _noinline2, _dontinline, _dontinline2, _dontinline3, _inline0, and _inline1 for codegen-sensitive wrappers and local helper variants.
Local anonymous helpers retained short decomp-style names such as inlineL0, inlineL1, inlineK0 where semantic names were not established.
Header comments preserve address offsets beside static prototypes, e.g. /* 09F198 */ static s32 ftCo_800A229C(Fighter* fp, Vec3*).
Existing named enums/helpers were reused when available, including CpuCmd_* commands, InternalStageId values, ItemKind values, and GA_Ground/GA_Air.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added noinline/dontinline wrapper chains around simple calls to force non-inlined call shapes at sensitive sites.
Used PAD_STACK in several functions to preserve stack layout.

Kept exact float constants and thresholds visible in C, including 0.00001f epsilons, 1000.0f ray lengths, 0.2f and 0.4f blast-zone margins, 5.0f ledge offsets, and angle constants for CPU recovery inputs.
Used mpCheckFloor, mpCheckLeftWall, and mpCheckRightWall with explicit line_id, flags, floor_pos, and floor_normal temporaries to reproduce collision query calling patterns.
Used switch statements over stage_info.internal_stage_id and CPU state fields where they matched large dispatch tables.
Used helper-specific sqrtf/sqrtf__Ff and small inline distance helpers where the diff shows codegen-sensitive math forms.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice; the PR body was empty/null.
decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code at 68.42%, an improvement of +0.19% and +7324 bytes.

Bot reported 11 new 100% matches: ftCo_800B21C8, ftCo_800B17D0, ftCo_800B1AB8, ftCo_800AEFB8, ftCo_800B1EF0, ftCo_800B24B8, ftCo_800AECF0, ftCo_800A648C, ftCo_800A4768, ftCo_800A4E8C, and ftCo_800B0E98.

Bot reported 25 improvements in unmatched items; largest listed gains included ftCo_800B2AFC (+2021 bytes to 90.71%), ftCo_800ADE48 (+1825 to 75.92%), ftCo_800A3908 (+1586 to 86.20%), and ftCo_800A4038 (+1453 to 79.01%).
Bot reported one regression: ftCo_800A2718 lost 22 matched bytes, moving from 82.34% to 77.32%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftCo_800A2718 to see whether the -22 byte regression was recovered.
Search subsequent ftCo_0A01 PRs for completion of partial matches listed by the bot, especially ftCo_800B2AFC, ftCo_800ADE48, ftCo_800A3908, ftCo_800A4038, ftCo_800A6FC4, and ftCo_800ABBA8.
Look for future renames or struct documentation for Fighter_x1A88_t fields used by the CPU AI state machine.
Search for reuse of ftCo_800A1B38_noinline, ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline2, and other wrapper tactics in nearby ftCommon matching work.
Trace stage helper declarations for grCastle_801CDF54, grGreatBay_801F66A4, grLib_801C9E60, and Camera_8003118C when decompiling more recovery/bounds code.
